subject: DevOps
title: Auto-Scaling Patterns
description: A fast breakdown of Auto-Scaling Patterns for builders.

hook.text: Black Friday hit 10x load. Without auto-scaling, the site crashed. With it, 40 servers launched.

buildup.text: Horizontal scaling adds instances. Vertical makes them bigger. Cloud favors horizontal for cost.

discovery.text: Scaling policies: add 2 instances when CPU tops 70%. Remove them when it drops below 30%.

twist.text: Predictive scaling reads history. If traffic spikes at 9 AM daily, it pre-scales at 8:55.

climax.text: K8s HPA scales pods. Cloud auto-scaling groups scale VMs. Same principle, different layers.

punchline.text: Scale out when busy, scale in when quiet. Match demand.

quiz.question: What advantage does predictive scaling have over reactive scaling?
options: It pre-provisions capacity before the traffic spike arrives
It costs less money than reactive scaling
It doesn't require any monitoring data
